name: Aelphia's Tomb
description:
This room hangs heavy with the stench of old perfumes. Several dried grain
husks adorn the walls, and a coffin of solid marble has been set into the
floor. This is the burial chamber of Aelphia, most favored wife of Tutankahmen
During her life she was a symbol of livelihood and fertility to the Egyptian
people as she bore Tutankahmen many children.

exits.description: It is too dark to see.
